197 def _drive_sonicwall_shell(shell, command: str, password: str = "", *,
198                            banner_wait: float = 2.0, drain_wait: float = 2.0,
199                            cmd_wait: float = 5.0, settle: float = 0.5,
200                            max_idle: int = 3) -> str:
201     """
202     Drive an interactive SonicOS shell and return the raw output of ``command``.
203 
204     SonicOS prints a login banner that re-prompts for the password (see below)
205     and pages long output by default. paramiko's interactive shell lets us
206     complete the shell-side password handshake, DISABLE paging with a separate
207     ``no cli pager session`` command (draining its response), then issue the
208     command and read the full reply — long ARP tables come back complete with no
209     ``--More--`` truncation. The generic Netmiko driver mis-times all of this.
210 
211     Double password: SonicWall asks for the password AGAIN on the interactive
212     shell even though paramiko already authenticated the SSH session (the banner
213     ends with "Access denied\nPassword:"). When that prompt is seen we re-send
214     the same password before continuing. This is normal SonicOS behavior; both
215     prompts take the same password.
216     """
217     # Read the login banner / initial prompt left in the channel after auth.
218     time.sleep(banner_wait)
219     banner = ""
220     if shell.recv_ready():
221         banner = shell.recv(65535).decode("utf-8", errors="ignore")
222 
223     # SonicWall re-prompts for the password on the interactive shell.
224     if "Password:" in banner or "Access denied" in banner:
225         shell.send(password + "\n")
226         time.sleep(banner_wait)
227         if shell.recv_ready():
228             shell.recv(65535)  # drain the post-login prompt
229 
230     # Disable CLI paging as its own command so long tables aren't truncated by
231     # --More--, then drain its response before issuing the real command.
232     shell.send("no cli pager session\n")
233     time.sleep(drain_wait)
234     if shell.recv_ready():
235         shell.recv(65535)  # drain the pager-disable response
236 
237     # Paging is now off — issue the command and read the full reply.
238     shell.send(command + "\n")
239     time.sleep(cmd_wait)
240 
241     output = ""
242     idle = 0
243     while True:
244         if shell.recv_ready():
245             output += shell.recv(65535).decode("utf-8", errors="ignore")
246             idle = 0
247             time.sleep(settle)
248         else:
249             idle += 1
250             if idle >= max_idle:
251                 break
252             time.sleep(settle)
253     return output
254
